address: 52 Sandy Lane, CV6 3FZ, Coventry (portfolio of 6 STL properties)
lead_profile_text: Experienced landlord with 6 short-let properties in Coventry near city centre, Coventry University, and Warwick University catchments. Current manager (Nice Living Accommodations) winding down due to double council tax penalty. 2 properties returning end of June 2026, 1 more in October, potentially 3 more early 2027. Properties fully furnished and already live on Airbnb/Booking.com. Phone call only — no Granola recording.
emotional_profile: Confident, experienced operator — not a first-time STL landlord. Motivated by better returns and stability after current manager's collapse. Solution-focused; does not need hand-holding. Portfolio migration is the key dynamic: first 2 properties are the gateway to all 6.
delay_trigger_phrases: I'd want to start with 2 and see how it goes
Properties are 90–95% ready — Zac can check the listings
I'll send you the listing links
delay_timeline: First 2 properties end of June 2026; 3rd October 2026; remaining 3 early 2027
green_light_trigger_phrases: They're winding down — facing the double council tax penalty
I'd want to start with 2 and see how it goes
I'll send you the listing links

top_objections.text: Mild uncertainty about decorating/touch-up costs before handover
top_objections.zac_response: Properties 90–95% ready; Zac to review listings first before determining any additional preparation needed.

top_questions.text: How does council tax work for STL properties — can we reduce the liability?
top_questions.answer: Switch one property from residential council tax to business rates — should result in zero liability. Second property: council tax remains but business rates are cheaper than residential rate.

top_questions.text: What is the best tax structure for extracting STL income?
top_questions.answer: Setting up a trading company for STL income extraction recommended — more tax-efficient than personal income.

what_moved_them: Market analysis showing low competition (avg 4.2 reviews, ~20 reviews per comparable listing)
Council tax/business rates restructuring — directly addresses a pain point with Nice Living
Trading company structure for tax-efficient income extraction
13% offer clearly valued as a meaningful reduction from 15%
Good direct booking opportunity via university and hospital proximity

conversion_gaps.gap: Portfolio migration is staged — only 2 of 6 properties immediately available
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Deliver exceptional performance on first 2 properties. Fast onboarding and clean handover from Nice Living are the critical success factors for triggering automatic migration of remaining 4.

offer_framing: 13%+VAT (from 15%), expires 25 June. No onboarding fee. Council tax restructuring and trading company setup as high-value differentiators beyond the management fee.
zac_close_phrase: Start with the 2 returning properties before 25 June to lock in 13% — migrate the rest as performance proves out
zac_income_intro: Net to owner averaging £2,100/month for 52 Sandy Lane. Worst month £1,500; best month £2,800. Nightly ~£250, occupancy ~54%. No month expected to be unprofitable. Year 2 annual net ~£25,200.
zac_transition_phrases: Start with 2 and migrate the full portfolio based on performance
Nice Living's collapse is your opportunity — clean handover, same properties, better management
zac_reassurance_phrases: No month expected to be unprofitable
No onboarding fee — we can get the listings transferred without any upfront cost
